certificate.caveat: No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys).
certificate.description: The Kiteworks Private Content Network (PCN) empowers organizations to share sensitive content with trusted parties by email, file sharing, file transfer, and other channels at the highest levels of security, governance, and compliance. The Kiteworks Cryptographic Module is an integral component of Kiteworks PCN, making it compatible for environments with strict cryptography requirements.

rows: Using the Output of a Random Bit Generator
Generation of Key Pairs for Asymmetric-Key Algorithms

KAS:KAS-ECC-SSC provides between 112 and 256 bits of encryption strength; KAS-FFC- SSC provides between 112 and 200 bits of encryption strength; KAS-IFC-SSC provides between 112 and 200 bits of encryption strength
KTS:2048, 3072, 4096 or 6144- bit keys provide between 112 and 176 bits of encryption strength
KTS (Cipher w/ CMAC, GMAC, HMAC, KMAC)
KTS:128, 192 or 256-bit keys provide between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength
KTS:128, 192 or 256-bit keys provide between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength
The strength of the Data Protection Key is based on the strength of the Password and/or Passphrase used in key derivation. SP 800-132 does not impose
any strictly defined requirements on the strength of a password. It says that “passwords should be strong enough so that it is infeasible for attackers to
get access by guessing a password.”
